---
title: "Syllabus"
---

## Workshop Description

*A short paragraph describing the workshop: what single cell RNA-seq is, who the workshop is intended for, the prerequisites (e.g. basic familiarity with R and the command line), and what participants should expect to be able to do by the end.*

## Workshop Structure

*Describe the structure: number of days/weeks, balance between lecture and hands-on, where slides and exercises live, how participants should prepare for each session.*

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this workshop, participants will be able to:

-   *Objective 1 — e.g. describe the biology and measurement process underlying single cell RNA-seq data.*
-   *Objective 2 — e.g. perform quality control, filtering, and normalization on a scRNA-seq count matrix.*
-   *Objective 3 — e.g. carry out dimensionality reduction and clustering, and interpret the results.*
-   *Objective 4 — e.g. identify marker genes and annotate cell types.*
-   *Objective 5 — e.g. perform differential expression between groups of cells.*
-   *Objective 6 — e.g. integrate multiple datasets and correct for batch effects.*

## Assessments / Exercises

*Describe how exercises are structured, whether they are graded, and how participants should submit them if at all.*

### *Grading (if applicable)*

| Assignment   | Percentage of Total Grade |
|--------------|:-------------------------:|
| *Exercise 1* |           *%*             |
| *Exercise 2* |           *%*             |
| *Project*    |           *%*             |
